definition: Cluster molecules by fingerprint similarity with Butina-style clustering. Treat shared cluster membership as high train-eval overlap, or use cluster-derived folds as discrete novelty groups.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --group-col cluster_id

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening on cancer cell lines

evidence.summary: The paper describes Butina clustering as a split designed to create dissimilar molecule clusters from molecular fingerprints.
The paper notes Butina clusters based on molecular fingerprint similarity but can have weak cluster quality relative to UMAP.
The paper reports Butina splits reduce train-neighbor Tanimoto similarity more than random or scaffold splits but less than UMAP splits.

limitations: Cluster quality can be poor with many singletons or weak separation.
A cluster split is not a continuous novelty curve unless paired with nearest-train similarity.
Threshold choices can materially change cluster membership.
quality_gates: Report clustering threshold and fingerprint configuration.
Report cluster-size distribution and singleton rate.
Validate with max train-neighbor similarity, not only cluster labels.
